final_explanation:
Based on the AI model's prediction,
The fact that the checking account balance is negative, because of immediate liquidity constraints, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[1]
The fact that the savings account balance is very low, because of limited liquid assets, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk.[2]
The fact that the applicant lacks a checking account, because of absent checking account status, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a high risk of default.

evidence_sentences.sentence: Applicants with a checking account balance below 0 DM (Deutsche Mark) exhibit a substantially higher estimated risk relative to the reference group, while the category of no checking account is associated with a lower estimated risk in this
while the category of no checking account is associated with a lower estimated risk in this dataset.

there is only a weak link between a bank’s exposure to interest rate risk and the change in its net interest income given an interest rate shock, which may be due to relatively low interest rate risk exposure of the banks in the sample.
evidence_sentences.source: Interest and credit risk management in German banks.pdf

evidence_sentences.sentence: The reduced model therefore retains only variables that demonstrate statistical relevance or clear theoretical importance in credit-risk modeling.

He further assumes that banks try to stabilize their future net interest income, i. e. to minimize the variance of C.NIMt,i, min IRRi var /parenleft.vsize1C.NIMt,i/parenright.vsize1= /parenleft.vsize1β1,t ⋅ θi + β2,t ⋅ IRRi/parenright.vsize12 var(ΔR) + σ2 ε (8) Taking θi as given and only being able to vary the exposure to interest rate risk IRRi, he obtains a relationship between θi and IRRi, namely that these two variables are associated in a positive way.
evidence_sentences.source: Interest and credit risk management in German banks.pdf

evidence_sentences.source: The Application of Tree-based model to Unbalanced German Credit Data Analysis.pdf

evidence_sentences.sentence: Even after exclusions, the reduced OLS model still shows limited explanatory power, with an R2 of 0.24 (Adj.R 2 =0.22) and a lower but still elevated condition number (=3.78×10 4).
while the category of no checking account is associated with a lower estimated risk in this dataset.

final_explanation_ko:
본 AI가 예측한 결과에 따르면,\n계좌 잔액 부족은 유동성 제약으로 인해 신용 위험을 증가시키는 신호로 알려져 있으며, 본 모델 역시 이를 위험 증가 요인으로 판단합니다[1].
저축 잔액이 거의 없는 것은 유동 자산이 제한적이라는 이유로 신용 위험을 높이는 신호로 인식되지만, 본 모델은 이를 위험 감소 요인으로 해석합니다[2].
문헌에서 입출금 계좌가 없는 것은 계좌 상태 부재로 인해 신용 위험을 낮추는 신호로 알려져 있지만, 본 모델은 이를 위험 증가 요인으로 판단합니다[3].
전반적으로 신용 위험이 높은 수준으로 평가되어 대출 승인 가능성이 낮습니다.
